summary: BA's analysis is mostly sound: factual claims about hook contents, citation accuracy, and scope boundaries are well-supported by direct file reads (verified). The user's '全修' is correctly decomposed into 3 in-scope work items (PATCH-1 bypass + LINE_LIMIT restore + PATCH-2 bypass) plus 1 explicitly out-of-scoped workflow-gate observation with rationale. However, the spec has FIVE internal inconsistencies — one BLOCKER and three MAJOR — that would cause Dev to ship the wrong code or QA to fail AC verification. The most serious is a hard contradiction in PATCH-1 sketch about where to insert the bypass branch (BEFORE vs BETWEEN is_exempt), plus AC3 promising subagent_id/agent_path alias coverage that the existing is_exempt() helper does not provide and that PATCH-1 explicitly forbids changing. AC2's substring loophole reintroduces the very asymmetry BA logged as out-of-scope. EnterWorktree prose tells subagents to use /do/allow but PATCH-2 wraps the bypass under `IS_SUB != 1`. BA must revise before Dev proceeds.

scope_alignment_evidence.user_universal_claim: 全修 (fix everything found by prior Codex audit re /do and /allow bypass)
codex_findings_inventory: Drift: LINE_LIMIT=1000 in code vs 600 in docstring + CLAUDE.md
Docs/code mismatch: pretool-block-enterworktree.sh unconditional block contradicts CLAUDE.md /do carve-out

ba_coverage.FINDING-1: covered by M1 + M2 (PATCH-1 bypass branches)
ba_coverage.FINDING-2: explicitly out_of_scope with documented rationale matching Codex's own classification ('I would not classify this as the same strict bug') — no subagent-parity gap exists, CLAUDE.md does not document /do as bypassing workflow checklists. Acceptable narrowing.

scope_alignment_evidence.narrowing_verdict: Acceptable. BA covers all four codex findings; the workflow-gate exclusion has explicit rationale that matches Codex's own classification and the user's documented /do contract in CLAUDE.md. No silent scope shrinkage.

pretool_read_size_guard_py.method: Direct Read of /root/.claude/hooks/pretool-read-size-guard.py:1-75; confirmed LINE_LIMIT=1000 at line 17, agent_id at line 24, imports lines 10-12, no Path/allowlist import

codex_audit_findings.claim: 1 strict bug + 1 contract ambiguity + LINE_LIMIT drift + EnterWorktree mismatch
codex_audit_findings.method: Read /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-1114740-1779202782.txt tail (line 7984+); confirmed all four findings with verbatim Codex framing

investigation_completeness_verified.verdict: BA did not just trust the audit; BA independently confirmed each affected file by direct Read. Component chain accurate. Reference source idioms correctly cited.

affected_file_accuracy_verified.details: Both PATCH-1 file (pretool-read-size-guard.py) and PATCH-2 file (pretool-block-enterworktree.sh) are the actual files Codex audit identified as broken. BA's diagnosis of which lines need editing matches what's there.

objections.claim: AC3 promises that 'truthy agent_id (or any of the SUBAGENT_ID_KEYS aliases)' triggers subagent exemption in the patched hook.
objections.problem: The existing is_exempt() function at pretool-read-size-guard.py:24 ONLY checks `data.get('agent_id')` — it does NOT check subagent_id, agent_path, parent_agent_id, or any other SUBAGENT_ID_KEYS alias. PATCH-1's Technical Hints explicitly forbid touching is_exempt() (line 238: 'Do NOT touch is_exempt()'). A QA test feeding a payload with `subagent_id` (or any alias other than agent_id) but no `agent_id` would FAIL AC3 because the hook still proceeds to line-count enforcement. This is an internal contradiction between AC3 and the patch specification.
objections.required_evidence: Either (a) narrow AC3 to truthy `agent_id` ONLY (matching what is_exempt actually checks today), or (b) change PATCH-1 to use lib.subagent.is_subagent_context(data) for the subagent check (which would touch is_exempt and conflict with M6's no-regression guarantee). Recommend (a) — narrow AC3.
objections.verified_via: Read /root/.claude/hooks/pretool-read-size-guard.py:21-25 (agent_id only); Read /root/.claude/hooks/lib/subagent.py:11-25 (SUBAGENT_ID_KEYS = agent_id, subagent_id, agent_path, parent_agent_id); cross-checked AC3 text at ticket-20260519-151734.md:164 ('any of the SUBAGENT_ID_KEYS aliases').

objections.claim: AC2 says the grant body matches `{"pattern": "Read", "is_regex": false}` OR 'any pattern that read_grant("Read", sid) matches as True'. AC7 has the parallel 'matching {"pattern": "EnterWorktree", ...} such that read_grant returns True' wording.
objections.problem: The 'or any pattern that read_grant matches' clause re-opens the substring/exact asymmetry BA explicitly logged as out-of-scope observation #2 (ticket-20260519-151734.md:145; context-20260519-151734.json:81-84). Under PreToolUse exact_or_substr semantics, `/allow Re` would substring-match 'Read' and pass AC2; but the same grant fails PostToolUse exact-only consumption (lib/allowlist.py:280-292 branch 3) and leaks the grant. BA's codex_consult feedback finding #7 claims AC2/AC7 'now specify exact-match grants', but the literal AC text contradicts that. This is a `spec_text_vs_execution_drift` situation: the AC literal is broader than what BA's own out-of-scope note says is safe to verify.
objections.required_evidence: Update AC2 to require exact literal `{"pattern": "Read", "is_regex": false}` ONLY (remove the 'or any pattern' clause). Update AC7 to require exact literal `{"pattern": "EnterWorktree", "is_regex": false}` ONLY. Keep the read_grant substring asymmetry in out_of_scope_observations as adjacent-issue OBSERVATION_ONLY.
objections.verified_via: Read ticket-20260519-151734.md:157, 190 (AC2/AC7 text); cross-checked context-20260519-151734.json:81-84 (out-of-scope observation #2 confirming asymmetry); cross-checked codex_consult finding #7 at context-20260519-151734.json:391-396 (claims exact-match but spec text says otherwise).

objections.claim: Ticket says 'Subagents that legitimately need EnterWorktree must also pass through /do or /allow, OR — more typically — use ~/.claude/scripts/create-worktree.sh' (line 51) and Risk 4 reiterates 'Subagents that legitimately need EnterWorktree should either use the documented ~/.claude/scripts/create-worktree.sh ... or pass through /do/allow like the main agent' (line 136).
objections.problem: PATCH-2 sketch wraps BOTH bypass branches (/do and /allow) inside `if [ "$_BEW_IS_SUB" != "1" ]; then ... fi` (lines 255-280). A subagent payload (agent_id present) would fall through to the existing block message. So a subagent CANNOT pass through /do or /allow under this PATCH — only the main agent can. The prose contradicts the patch behavior. This will trigger 'no PROPOSED_FIX' churn in /close debate when a reviewer asks: how does a subagent use /do here?
objections.required_evidence: Either (a) revise the prose at lines 51 and 136 to: 'Subagents remain unconditionally blocked by this hook (PATCH-2 is main-agent-only by design); subagents needing EnterWorktree must use ~/.claude/scripts/create-worktree.sh — no /do/allow path is provided for subagents in this cycle' (matches PATCH-2 behavior), OR (b) revise PATCH-2 to also honor /do/allow for subagents (would expand the scope and require a new AC). Recommend (a) — narrow the prose.
objections.verified_via: Read ticket-20260519-151734.md:51 ('Reconciliation' paragraph end), :136 (Risk 4); Read PATCH-2 sketch at :251-281 (IS_SUB != 1 wrapper); confirmed the bypass exits sit INSIDE the wrapper, no subagent path exists.

objections.claim: PATCH-1 Technical Hints sketch (lines 230-237) has two contradictory placement instructions for the new bypass branch.
objections.problem: Line 230: 'In main(), after data = json.load(sys.stdin) and after extracting tool_input and file_path, but BEFORE the existing is_exempt(...) call, insert: ...' Line 237: 'The placement is intentional: it sits BETWEEN the subagent exemption (is_exempt at line 21-25) and the line-count enforcement.' These cannot both be true. BEFORE is_exempt means the bypass runs first — but then the bypass code itself checks `not data.get('agent_id')` (line 232), which makes the subagent exemption inside is_exempt irrelevant for Read. BETWEEN is_exempt and line_count means AFTER is_exempt — but then is_exempt already returned True for binary files and non-existent files, so the bypass for those cases is moot, AND the agent_id check in the bypass body becomes redundant with is_exempt's agent_id check at line 24. A Dev reading this will pick one interpretation and may pick wrong. Either reading is technically defensible but they verify different ACs.
objections.required_evidence: Pick ONE placement and rewrite both prose lines to match. Recommended: place the bypass AFTER `if is_exempt(data, file_path): sys.exit(0)` (the BETWEEN interpretation), because (i) it keeps the subagent agent_id exemption as the authoritative first check; (ii) it preserves the binary-file and non-existent-file fast paths; (iii) the redundant `not data.get('agent_id')` in the bypass body becomes harmless defensive code. Rewrite line 230 to say 'AFTER the existing is_exempt(...) call but BEFORE the line_count = count_lines(file_path) line'. Delete or align line 237.
objections.verified_via: Read ticket-20260519-151734.md:230, :237; Read pretool-read-size-guard.py:46-58 (main() flow showing is_exempt then line_count); the two AC placement-implied behaviors verify differently for binary-file payloads and missing-file payloads.

objections.claim: AC9 says 'the 8 hooks listed by the Codex audit' then enumerates 9 file names then says 'no changes to any of those 9 files'.
objections.problem: Counting bug: the enumeration lists 9 names (pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-write-guard.sh, pretool-subagent-enforce.py, pretool-aggregate-check.py, prompt-workflow.py, userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh — that's 9). The opening 'the 8 hooks' claim is wrong; the closing 'those 9 files' is right. Avoidable /close-debate nit that signals BA didn't proofread AC9.
objections.required_evidence: Either drop one name from the list (e.g., stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh is housekeeping, not a /do-honoring hook, so could be moved out), or change the opening to '9 files' and keep the list as-is. Recommend the second — list and count both match.
objections.verified_via: Counted file names in ticket-20260519-151734.md:203; cross-checked codex audit which listed 8 already-honoring hooks PLUS 2 housekeeping files (stop-cleanup, userprompt-consent) — codex grouped them as 8+2, BA collapsed to one list.

non_objection_areas.evidence_quality: PASS for all factual claims about file contents, line numbers, and idiom citations. BA quoted has_consent and read_grant verbatim.
non_objection_areas.scope_alignment: PASS for the four-codex-findings coverage. workflow-gate out-of-scope decision has rationale matching Codex's own classification.
non_objection_areas.investigation_completeness: PASS. BA read 5 hook files end-to-end (read-size-guard, block-enterworktree, allowlist, orchestrator-gate, write-guard) and quoted accurately.
non_objection_areas.affected_file_accuracy: PASS. Both PATCH-1 and PATCH-2 target the actual broken files.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex CLI (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) returned 5 concrete adversarial findings — 1 BLOCKER, 3 MAJOR, 1 MINOR — all with PROPOSED_FIX. All 5 confirmed by independent QA verification against the BA spec text. Findings: (1) AC3 alias mismatch vs is_exempt actual behavior; (2) AC2/AC7 substring loophole vs out-of-scope observation; (3) EnterWorktree subagent prose contradicts PATCH-2 IS_SUB wrapper; (4) PATCH-1 placement contradiction (BEFORE vs BETWEEN is_exempt); (5) AC9 8-vs-9 count nit.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 codex findings converted to objections OBJ-1 through OBJ-5 with verification evidence and required-evidence guidance. QA's original draft PASS verdict flipped to FAIL on the strength of OBJ-1 (BLOCKER) alone; OBJ-2/3/4 (MAJOR) each independently support FAIL. No codex findings discarded as OBSERVATION_ONLY because all are concrete spec defects, not nitpicks. Codex output archived at /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-2931050-1779205159.txt.

recommendation: Re-invoke BA with the 5 objections. Required minimal revisions: (1) AC3 narrow to 'truthy agent_id' only [BLOCKER]; (2) AC2/AC7 remove 'or any pattern that read_grant matches' [MAJOR]; (3) Lines 51 + 136 prose rewrite: subagents remain blocked, must use create-worktree.sh [MAJOR]; (4) Technical Hints PATCH-1 placement: pick AFTER is_exempt, rewrite both prose lines consistently [MAJOR]; (5) AC9 fix count: '9 files' [MINOR]. Once BA revises, Dev can proceed safely. Without these fixes, Dev will either (a) ship code that fails AC3 on subagent_id payloads, (b) verify against ambiguous AC text that hides the substring asymmetry, or (c) be unable to deterministically pick PATCH-1 insertion point.

refined_context.recommended_approach: BA re-invocation with the 5 specific revision asks above. No new investigation required — all evidence already in the spec and the hook files. The fixes are pure text edits to the BA spec and one Technical Hints clarification.
